families.note: Typed London mulligan, keep, and ordered bottom options are live; sealed benchmark fixtures remain absent.

Every live human and AI priority window uses one shared canonical context with a forced-pass fast path, exact normal-cost instant choices, and typed program-bound non-mana activations. Activation targets, optional answers, and payments survive the stack; alternate costs, unsupported extra activation costs, and triggered-ability prompts remain open.

Human and AI consume the same canonical land, mana activation, typed program-bound non-mana activation, normal-cost cast, and finish options. Descriptors bind exact targets, modes, optional answers, and every legal payment. Variable/additional/alternate costs and spell or trigger resolution-time object choices remain open.

Human and AI consume the same capped canonical Cartesian product assigning each legal attacker to no attack or any legal live player defender, including split multiplayer attacks; non-player defenders and sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.

Human and AI consume complete capped canonical blocker combinations for every attacked player in deterministic APNAP order, with declarations accumulated by the kernel; strategic damage ordering and sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.
The live human and AI paths enumerate, validate, dispatch, replay, and record both canonical move-or-leave options; sealed benchmark fixtures remain absent.

Normal spells and typed program-bound activated abilities enumerate exact legal target products and preserve selected targets through stack resolution. Triggered targets, target distribution, and partial-illegality resolution remain open.
Normal modal spell branches are canonical, visible to humans, available to AI, hashed on the stack, and bound at resolution. Modal activated and triggered abilities remain open.
Typed IDs exist; no complete production prompt adapter is claimed.
Current main-phase options bind explicit mana plans; general strategic payment prompts remain open.

Normal spell and typed program-bound activation optional-effect products are canonical, visible to humans, available to AI, hashed on the stack, and bound at resolution. Optional costs and triggered or deferred optional prompts remain open.
The current diagnostics driver permits only autonomous triggers.

Bounded interpreter object-choice products now use one canonical context for activated and triggered resolution, including authorized human labels and real AI successor actions. Non-library coverage, multi-zone choices, trigger targets, and teacher-corpus fixtures remain incomplete.

Typed program-bound activated and triggered abilities now defer object selection until successful resolution, enumerate exact bounded legal products including fail-to-find, filter through the interpreter's authoritative predicate, expose authorized labels to humans, evaluate real bound actions for AI, and replay canonically. Spell-resolution, trigger-target, multi-zone, and sealed benchmark coverage remain open.
Automatic legal damage is exercised, but strategic order and assignment prompts remain open.
The canonical descriptor maps to an immediate typed kernel concession with canonical events; live human, AI, and benchmark prompt adapters remain open.
